survivors_flagged.one_line: SGT-003 Duchenne: mid-2026 INSPIRE interim data update + FDA accelerated-approval pathway guidance expected; dated-ish biotech binary, modest asymmetry, Phase 3 already dosing.
Phase 3 REGAL AML at 78/80 events as of May 11 — final OS readout imminent (triggers at 80th event); genuine near-term dated binary, $135.8M cash de-risks, mispriced asymmetry on a micro-cap.
Ivonescimab HARMONi BLA PDUFA Nov 14, 2026 (US EGFR-NSCLC); dated binary but well-known/far out and pivotal HARMONi-3 PFS not until 1H2027.
Dated Jul 14 2026 R&D Day + multiple 2026 Revuforj/Niktimvo data readouts, but commercial-launch/data growth not asymmetric special-sit binary.
Live binary: federal DPA-ordered Santa Ynez restart vs CA-state injunction noncompliance ruling; first-sales pushed, active court fight w/ mispriced asymmetry, unresolved.

Live Elevidys regulatory overhang post-2025 liver-failure deaths plus an accepted efficacy supplement to expand/restore the label; a real but diffuse, multi-step regulatory binary (not one clean dated PDUFA), partly already re-rated.
Apitegromab SMA BLA resubmitted with hard PDUFA Sept 30, 2026; prior CRL was fill-finish manufacturing only (efficacy/safety intact), so the dated binary carries mispriced approval asymmetry.

Getty merger-of-equals with cash/stock election; DOJ cleared unconditionally and UK CMA conditionally cleared May 15 2026 requiring editorial (Rex/Backgrid/Splash) divestiture, close expected H2 2026 — a real complicated merger-arb still open.
Prosperity Bancshares stock-and-cash acquisition — ALL regulatory + shareholder approvals in, expected CLOSE on/about July 1 2026 (imminent dated merger-arb closing).
Molgramostim BLA, PDUFA Nov 22, 2026 — would be first-ever FDA therapy for aPAP (Breakthrough/Priority, no safety/efficacy concerns cited); clean dated binary with asymmetry.
Definitive all-stock onsemi merger (1.350 ON/share) but close not until mid-2027 — distant, fixed-ratio equity deal, thin arb edge.
Definitive UHS all-cash deal at $5.25 (vs $5.20); holders approved May 29, only state regulatory approvals left, close Q3 2026 — clean near-dated arb.
Definitive Zymeworks all-cash deal at $17.00 (vs $17.06) + CVR on ampreloxetine; close 2H 2026 — cash arb with free option kicker.
IKS all-cash deal at $26.25 (vs $26.18); HSR cleared June 22, shareholder vote July 7 2026 — imminent, multiple gates passed.

verified.catalyst: SGT-003 Duchenne: an imminent mid-2026 update bundling fresh INSPIRE DUCHENNE interim data with FDA guidance (from H1-2026 meetings) on a potential accelerated-approval pathway.
verified.dated_milestone: "Mid-2026" update on regulatory + clinical progress, incl. FDA accelerated-approval pathway feedback from H1-2026 meetings; next date ~late June/Q3 2026 (today is 2026-06-29, at the guided boundary).
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete commitment: scheduled FDA accelerated-approval pathway meetings/guidance in H1-2026 with a mid-2026 update) + G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: 60% Day-90 / 91% Day-360 microdystrophin expression as the surrogate-endpoint basis for an AA filing not yet blessed by FDA).
verified.edge: M — specific, idiosyncratic, currently-unpriced regulatory step (FDA AA-pathway blessing would de-risk a BLA on the microdystrophin surrogate), but the "mid-2026" date is soft (guidance/discussion, not a hard PDUFA or fixed data readout) and the DMD gene-therapy AA path carries class overhang (Sarepta/Elevidys), so asymmetry is modest per the triage.
verified.primary_source: Solid Biosciences GlobeNewswire press release, "Provides Interim Positive Clinical Update on Phase 1/2 INSPIRE DUCHENNE Trial," 2026-03-11 (corroborated by SLDB Q1 FY2026 Form 10-Q, period ended 2026-03-31).
verified.analysis: Verified via primary sources: Solid's 2026-03-11 release confirms 40 dosed in Phase 1/2 INSPIRE DUCHENNE with robust microdystrophin (60% Day-90, 91% Day-360) and explicit guidance to hold H1-2026 FDA meetings on a potential accelerated-approval pathway and "provide an update mid-2026"; Phase 3 IMPACT DUCHENNE is dosing (FDA-aligned 2026-02-09). The value-creating event — FDA endorsing the AA surrogate path / a disclosed BLA timeline — is still forward and unfired; no AA decision or BLA date has been disclosed as of the 2026-03-11 release and Q1 10-Q. Gate passes (real+specific G2/G3, dated-ish mid-2026 and imminent given today is 2026-06-29, forward, idiosyncratic not macro), but the soft date plus modest asymmetry and gene-therapy DMD class overhang keep it WATCH not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: SELLAS' pivotal Phase 3 REGAL trial of galinpepimut-S (GPS) in 2nd-line-remission AML triggers its final event-driven overall-survival readout at the 80th death — it stood at 78/80 as of May 11, 2026, with topline data expected imminently (summer 2026).
verified.dated_milestone: 80th-event final OS readout / topline data — next date: summer 2026 (event-driven; 78/80 as of May 11, 2026, only 2 events remaining)
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete pre-specified commitment: event-driven final OS analysis triggers at the 80th death, database-lock + unblinding + topline disclosure mechanically follows) + G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: positive interim showed median OS >=13.5 mo GPS vs ~6 mo SoC; the trial stalling at 78 events for ~7 weeks is itself a survival-benefit tell on the GPS arm that the tape under-weights on a sub-$300M micro-cap)
verified.edge: M — genuine dated, unfired binary with a passed positive interim (efficacy/futility/safety) and the slow event accrual cutting in the bull's favor; tempered because it is a true coin-flip binary (OS could still miss), the stock has already re-rated hard (+50% on the near-trigger news) compressing the easy asymmetry, and overhangs exist (GPS China-payment arbitration, change-of-control filing, record short interest)
verified.primary_source: SELLAS Q1 2026 financial results / corporate update, "78 events have occurred in the Trial as of May 11, 2026; Final Analysis to be Conducted Following the 80th Event" (StockTitan/ir.sellaslifesciences.com, dated 2026-05-12)
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts confirmed vs primary sources (company GlobeNewswire Q1 PR 2026-05-12, SEC 8-K): 78 of 80 REGAL OS events as of May 11, 2026; cash $107.1M at 3/31/26 + $7.5M Q2 warrant proceeds (~$114.6M); IDMC 'continue without modification.' NOT FIRED — no 80th-event/topline announcement as of late June 2026; final analysis mechanically imminent (CEO: 'a few weeks' after 80th event). CORRECTIONS: (1) the median OS '>=13.5 mo GPS vs ~6 mo SoC' is a POOLED/BLINDED interim median vs an EXPECTED SoC baseline (CancerNetwork) — company is still blinded, so this is NOT a clean unblinded GPS-vs-SoC efficacy signal; 'de-risked' is slightly generous. (2) Overhangs confirmed: short interest ~32.65% of float / ~63.4M shares (June 15), change-of-control 8-K filed June 25; GPS China arbitration NOT independently verified. Remains a binary, still-blinded OS coin-flip with ~50% near-trigger re-rate already in the tape — score held at 7.
verified.analysis: REGAL is a real, pre-specified, event-driven Phase 3 OS readout: 78 of 80 required deaths as of May 11, 2026, so the final analysis is mechanically imminent (summer 2026) and is NOT yet fired — the 80th event had not been announced as of late June 2026. The setup is de-risked by a positive IDMC interim (median OS >=13.5 mo GPS vs ~6 mo SoC, continue-without-modification) and a strong balance sheet ($107.1M cash at 3/31/26 plus $7.5M warrant proceeds, ~$114.6M, comfortably funding through readout and BLA prep), and the unusually slow accrual of the last events favors longer survival in the active arm. Score held at 7 (not higher) because it remains a binary OS coin-flip that could still miss, the equity already re-rated ~50% on the near-trigger catalyst, and there are live overhangs (GPS China arbitration, change-of-control filing, all-time-high short interest).

verified.catalyst: FDA-accepted BLA for ivonescimab + chemo in EGFRm NSCLC post-TKI with a PDUFA goal date of Nov 14, 2026.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: U.S. FDA) + G2 (concrete commitment: filed BLA, mid-cycle/wrap-up review, PDUFA goal action date)
verified.edge: L — well-known and far out (~4.5 mo); the OS overhang already drove SMMT from $30.98 to ~$14.32 (below 50/200-day MAs). The pivotal HARMONi missed its OS endpoint (HR 0.79, p=0.057); only PFS was significant (HR 0.52). Approvability on PFS-only is debated and largely priced; the genuine re-rating catalyst (HARMONi-3 PFS / mature HARMONi-2 OS) sits in 1H2027 and is undated.
verified.primary_source: Summit Therapeutics 8-K / press release, FDA BLA acceptance with Nov 14, 2026 PDUFA (Feb 2026); HARMONi OS HR 0.79, p=0.057 per company disclosures (sec.gov / smmttx.com); FMP quote $14.32 (2026-06-29)
verified.analysis: Catalyst clears Bloom G1 (FDA named) and G2 (filed BLA, defined review, dated PDUFA Nov 14, 2026) — it is real, dated, and forward (not yet fired). But edge is Low: this is a heavily-followed, far-out binary on a BLA whose pivotal HARMONi trial missed OS (HR 0.79, p=0.057) and rests on PFS (HR 0.52), an overhang already reflected in a fall from $30.98 to ~$14.32 and an H.C. Wainwright downgrade. No specific currently-unpriced figure (fails G3); the data point that would actually move the franchise (HARMONi-3 PFS) is undated in 1H2027, so this stays WATCH, score 5, rather than ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: MAXPIRe Phase 2 topline of axatilimab/Niktimvo in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis — a new-indication proof-of-concept readout — is on track for Q4 2026, alongside frontline-AML revumenib data (EVOLVE-2/REVEAL-ND/BEAT AML) in 2H 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: MAXPIRe IPF Ph2 topline expected Q4 2026 (enrollment completed Jan 2026); frontline AML revumenib topline 2H 2026; R&D Day Jul 14 2026 (presentation only). Next dated event: R&D Day Jul 14 2026.
verified.instrument: equity (or Q4-dated calls to express the IPF Ph2 binary)
verified.bloom_gates: G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: axatilimab IPF Ph2 PoC in a multi-billion-$ indication the base-case valuation, built on approved GVHD/AML, does not credit; Orphan Drug Designation granted). Partial G2 (concrete enrolled/dated trials with set topline windows).
verified.edge: M — the Q4 2026 MAXPIRe IPF Ph2 readout is a genuine dated, forward, unpriced new-indication binary; but SNDX is a ~$3B well-covered commercial biotech and the AML/GVHD readouts are largely anticipated label-expansions, so asymmetry is moderate and concentrated in the single IPF PoC, not a clean special-sit.
verified.primary_source: Syndax IR / Q1 2026 business update (per ir.syndax.com, MAXPIRe enrollment completed Jan 2026, topline Q4 2026; frontline AML 2H 2026) and Syndax R&D Day press release dated Jun 1 2026 (globenewswire/ir.syndax.com), accessed 2026-06-29.
verified.analysis: The triage correctly dismissed the Jul 14 2026 R&D Day (it is an investor presentation, not a data event) but undercounted the real catalyst: the MAXPIRe Phase 2 of axatilimab in IPF completed enrollment Q1 2026 with topline on track for Q4 2026 — a dated, forward, not-yet-fired proof-of-concept in a large new indication the current valuation does not credit (G3), supported by FDA Orphan Drug Designation. Frontline-AML revumenib data (EVOLVE-2/REVEAL-ND/BEAT AML) is also dated for 2H 2026, but that is a partly-anticipated label expansion rather than asymmetric. Net: a real dated binary exists, so this clears NONE, but the edge is medium and concentrated in one Ph2 readout within an already richly-valued name — WATCH, score 5, revisit if pre-data the IPF optionality stays uncredited.

verified.catalyst: Federal DPA-ordered Santa Ynez Unit oil restart (active sales since Mar 2026) collides with a California state-court injunction Sable is defying, now an active court fight over whether the AG can halt the flow.
verified.dated_milestone: CA AG halt-motion hearing was set for June 1, 2026 (now lapsed); contempt/OSC track before Judge Geck (May 22 hearing held) plus federal appeals ongoing — next specific forward date NOT confirmed within budget.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparties: U.S. DOE/Trump DPA order, CA AG Rob Bonta, Judge Donna Geck) + G2 (concrete commitment: DPA restart directive executed, first oil sales Mar 30 2026, oil actively flowing)
verified.primary_source: Santa Barbara Independent, "California Attorney General Files Motion to Halt the Flow of Sable Oil," 2026-05-05 (June 1 hearing date); Center for Biological Diversity / Independent rulings, 2026-04-17 (noncompliance + DPA-does-not-shield finding); SOC first-sales press release 2026-03-30.
verified.analysis: SOC is a genuine live distressed binary: a March 2026 federal Defense Production Act order let Sable restart Santa Ynez transport and begin oil sales, but Santa Barbara Superior Court kept its injunction in force, ruled in April the DPA does not override state law (Hercules/Agent Orange precedent), found Sable in noncompliance, and the CA AG moved May 1 to halt the flow with a hearing set June 1. The asymmetry is real and mispriced — equity has collapsed to $6.97 (52w high $32) on litigation fear while oil is physically flowing and revenue is accruing — but the clean forward dated milestone (June 1) has lapsed as of today and I could not confirm the post-June ruling/next hearing within the lookup budget. Gating WATCH not ACTIVE: catalyst is real/specific/named but the forward date is stale-pending-refresh and the thesis leans heavily on an unpredictable court/regulatory outcome rather than a company-controlled event.

verified.catalyst: ENDEAVOR Cohort 8 sirolimus-prophylaxis readout that, if clean, supports a future FDA bid to restore Elevidys' non-ambulatory label (removed after the 2025 fatal-liver-failure deaths/boxed warning).
verified.dated_milestone: Cohort 8 primary-endpoint data collection expected 2H 2026 (per Q1 2026 call, 2026-05-06); dosing began ~Apr 2026. No dated PDUFA for label restoration.
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete FDA-agreed commitment: ENDEAVOR Cohort 8, ~25 non-ambulatory pts, defined peri-infusion sirolimus regimen); partial G3 (the unpriced restoration of the non-ambulatory DMD population is a real revenue figure, but undated/multi-step).
verified.edge: M — interim real-world sirolimus data show no liver-enzyme elevations and the non-ambulatory population is a sizable un-fired sleeve, but the path is a clinical readout then a separate undated FDA submission, not a single dated regulatory binary.
verified.primary_source: Sarepta Q1 2026 earnings call / Cohort 8 PR (2026-05-06); FDA boxed-warning & ambulatory-only label action (Jul/Aug 2025).
verified.analysis: The clean dated catalyst implied by the triage flag (the "accepted efficacy supplement") actually traces to the 2024 indication-expansion sBLA whose PDUFA already fired and was overtaken by the 2025 liver-failure deaths, boxed warning, ambulatory-only label restriction, and platform-designation revocation. The genuine forward event is now the ENDEAVOR Cohort 8 sirolimus study (dosing started ~Apr 2026, primary data collection targeted 2H 2026), but label restoration would require a subsequent undated FDA submission/concurrence — a diffuse, multi-step regulatory process, not one clean dated PDUFA, and the stock has already partly re-rated off the July-2025 shipping-resumption bottom. Real and specific but soft-dated and multi-step, so WATCH rather than ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Apitegromab SMA BLA accepted with a hard PDUFA action date of Sept 30, 2026, where the prior CRL was solely a Catalent fill-finish CMC observation (no efficacy/safety/drug-substance concerns), now remediated and de-risked via a second US fill-finish site.
verified.dated_milestone: FDA PDUFA action date Sept 30, 2026 (BLA resubmitted Mar 31, 2026; EMA MAA decision also expected mid-2026)
verified.instrument: equity (SRRK); pre-PDUFA Sept/Oct call spreads viable given dated binary and elevated IV
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: FDA action + Catalent Indiana/Novo Nordisk fill-finish remediation) and G3 (specific unpriced figure: gap between ~90% modeled approval PoS for a CMC-only remediation binary and the modest premium embedded in $54.96 spot vs $59.31 avg PT / $48 floor, against $1.8B modeled peak sales)
verified.edge: M — the asymmetry is real (purely fill-finish CRL + dual-site fix + Phase 3 SAPPHIRE already met its primary endpoint makes this a manufacturing-remediation binary, not a clinical one, so de-facto approval odds far exceed a typical PDUFA), but it is a heavily-followed Strong-Buy name (18 buys, PT only ~8% above spot), so the mispricing is contested rather than hidden; downside is a re-inspection slip/second CRL, not data failure.
verified.primary_source: Scholar Rock IR press release "Scholar Rock Resubmits BLA to FDA for Apitegromab" + Businesswire (Mar 31, 2026, PDUFA Sept 30, 2026); CRL nature per Scholar Rock IR "CRL Solely Related to Observations Identified at Catalent Indiana LLC Fill-Finish Facility" (Oct 2025) and rarediseaseadvisor.com; price/PT per Yahoo/MarketBeat (Jun 29, 2026)
verified.kill_fact: CRL date is wrong: the line says "Oct-2025 CRL" but Scholar Rock's own release (Businesswire) is dated Sept 23, 2025 — i.e. late September, not October. Everything else is independently confirmed forward and unfired: apitegromab BLA resubmitted Mar 31, 2026 and accepted with a firm PDUFA action date of Sept 30, 2026 (Cure SMA / Q1-2026 release); the CRL was "solely related to observations" at the Catalent Indiana LLC fill-finish facility with explicitly no efficacy/safety/drug-substance concerns; the resubmission added a second US fill-finish site after a positive Type C meeting (Mar 3, 2026); SAPPHIRE Phase 3 hit its primary endpoint. Live tradeable: SRRK $54.96, mkt cap $6.59B, 52wk 27.07–56.54. Residual risk is a Catalent re-inspection slip / second CMC CRL that delays (not destroys) — note resubmission was made pre-reinspection in alignment with FDA, which is a genuine open risk. Score held at 7: dated forward CMC-remediation binary on a de-risked asset, but well-covered Strong-Buy consensus = asymmetry acknowledged not hidden.
verified.analysis: Verified, dated, forward, unfired binary. Apitegromab's BLA was resubmitted Mar 31, 2026 and accepted with a firm Sept 30, 2026 PDUFA; the Oct-2025 CRL was confirmed by Scholar Rock's own release as "solely related to observations" at the Catalent Indiana fill-finish facility, with explicitly no efficacy/safety or drug-substance concerns, and the resubmission added a second US fill-finish site to de-risk. That makes this a CMC-remediation binary on a drug whose Phase 3 SAPPHIRE already hit its primary endpoint, so true approval odds sit well above a generic PDUFA while spot ($54.96, $6.59B cap) trades only modestly below the $59 avg / $70 high PT — genuine mispriced approval asymmetry. Edge is medium not high because it is a well-covered Strong-Buy consensus name (the asymmetry is acknowledged, not hidden) and the chief residual risk is a re-inspection slip or second CMC CRL that delays rather than destroys the asset.

verified.catalyst: Closing of the ~$3.7B Getty Images (GETY) merger-of-equals once the CMA-mandated editorial-business divestiture (Shutterstock Editorial/Backgrid/Splash) completes to an approved buyer.
verified.dated_milestone: CMA final undertakings to be accepted after the June 24, 2026 comment deadline; editorial divestiture-buyer approval pending; deal close expected H2 2026.
verified.instrument: SSTK common (merger-arb long); GETY common is the consideration leg / hedge short
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty GETY), G2 (concrete CMA divestiture commitment + dated undertakings/close), G3 (specific unpriced spread)
verified.primary_source: CMA Getty/Shutterstock merger inquiry final report, conditional clearance May 15 2026 + notice to accept final undertakings June 10 2026 (gov.uk); DOJ unconditional clearance Feb 23 2026 (investor.shutterstock.com); deal terms per S-4 / Jan 7 2025 announcement.
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts independently confirmed against primary/reliable sources. Live quotes match to the cent: SSTK $14.13, GETY $0.9401 (yearHigh $3.21, yearLow $0.582 — collapse + near-low both true). Blended deal value = 9.17 x $0.9401 + $9.50 = $18.12 vs SSTK $14.13 = ~28.2% gross spread, exactly as stated. Merger-of-equals ~$3.7B; proration fixes aggregate at $9.50 cash + 9.17 GETY shares/SSTK with NO cash collar, so cash electors prorate down and SSTK holders ride the floating GETY leg — confirmed via Getty/SSTK 8-K mechanics. DOJ unconditional clearance Feb 23 2026 (SSTK IR release); CMA final report May 15 2026 mandating FULL editorial-business divestiture (Shutterstock Editorial/Rex Features/Backgrid/Splash); CMA notice to accept final undertakings June 10, comment deadline 5pm June 24 2026 (gov.uk CMA case page); close expected H2 2026. Forward, un-fired, dated, deal-specific (not macro). Edge is the spread net of elevated completion/repricing risk (GETY near 52wk low, debt wall, floating leg), not a clean capture — score 7 fair.
verified.analysis: SSTK is a genuinely open, complicated merger-arb: GETY merger-of-equals ($28.85 cash / 13.67 GETY shares / blended 9.17 GETY + $9.50 cash, prorated), DOJ-cleared unconditionally (Feb 23 2026) and CMA-cleared conditionally (May 15 2026) requiring full divestiture of the editorial business, with final undertakings pending after the June 24 2026 comment deadline and close expected H2 2026 — clears all three Bloom gates, dated, forward, un-fired, deal-specific (not macro). The hook is the ~28% gross spread (blended $18.12 vs $14.13), but that spread is wide for cause: GETY has collapsed from $3.21 to $0.94 (near 52-wk low, debt-wall flagged), and there is no cash collar — cash elections prorate down to the fixed $9.50 aggregate, so SSTK holders ride the floating GETY leg. ACTIVE/7: real and verifiable, but the edge is the spread net of elevated completion/repricing risk, not a clean capture.

verified.catalyst: Prosperity Bancshares (PB) acquisition of Stellar Bancorp — all regulatory and shareholder approvals secured, closing on/about July 1, 2026 for $11.36 cash + 0.3803 PB shares per STEL share.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Prosperity Bancshares); G2/G3 fail — no concrete unpriced figure remains
verified.edge: L — spread is fully collapsed: implied deal value $11.36 + 0.3803×$73.52(PB) = $39.32 vs STEL $39.31, ~0% gross to a close ~2 days away; no incremental return left, only de-minimis carry/deal-break tail risk
verified.primary_source: PRNewswire / SEC 8-K — Prosperity & Stellar announce receipt of all regulatory approvals (2026-04-22); Stellar shareholders approve merger (8-K, 2026-05-27); both reaffirm close on/about July 1, 2026. Terms per 424B3 prospectus (d137365d424b3).
verified.analysis: The flag is factually accurate and well-corroborated: regulatory approvals (Fed Dallas waiver, FDIC, Texas DOB) landed 2026-04-22 and Stellar shareholders approved on 2026-05-27, with both parties reaffirming a close on/about July 1, 2026. However, this catalyst is effectively FIRED, not forward — the merger-arb spread has fully collapsed (implied $39.32 vs market $39.31, ~0% gross) with only ~2 trading days to a near-certain close, so there is no unpriced edge and only de-minimis deal-break tail risk remains. Fails the FORWARD/unpriced test (G3): real and dated (G1 satisfied) but the market has already re-rated STEL to the deal value, so it warrants tier NONE rather than ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: FDA decision on Savara's Molbreevi (molgramostim) BLA, which would be the first-ever approved therapy for autoimmune pulmonary alveolar proteinosis (aPAP).
verified.dated_milestone: PDUFA action date Nov 22, 2026 (extended from Aug 22, 2026)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: FDA) + G2 (concrete commitment: accepted BLA with fixed PDUFA goal date)
verified.primary_source: Savara press release / StockTitan + Managed Healthcare Executive, PDUFA extended to Nov 22, 2026 (announced Apr 15, 2026); JMP $9 PT / ~$750M peak (Investing.com)
verified.kill_fact: PDUFA Nov 22, 2026 confirmed (extended 3mo from original Aug 22, 2026 PDUFA, priority review); first-ever FDA aPAP therapy + Breakthrough/Priority/Orphan/Fast Track all confirmed; catalyst genuinely forward/unfired (~5mo out as of 2026-06-29), tradeable NASDAQ equity ~$1.1-1.17B cap, IMPALA-2 Ph3 (n=164) supports BLA. CORRECTIONS: extension was announced Apr 16, 2026 (line says Apr 15, off 1 day); FDA cited a request for 'additional information' with NO safety/efficacy/manufacturing concerns (line frames as 'major amendment' — directionally right that it's not a red-flag, but the specific 'major amendment' characterization is not what the company stated). G1/G2 satisfied; G3 partial (peak-sales are analyst estimates, not company guidance). Thesis intact: lone clinical binary, partly discounted, CRL punishing.
verified.analysis: SVRA is a single-asset binary on the Nov 22, 2026 PDUFA for inhaled GM-CSF molgramostim in aPAP, which would be the first FDA therapy for the disease and carries Breakthrough/Priority/Orphan/Fast Track tags; the Apr 15, 2026 three-month extension stemmed from a major amendment to the BLA, not any safety/efficacy/manufacturing concern, which de-risks the question mark somewhat. Catalyst is genuinely forward and unfired with a hard dated milestone, satisfying G1 (FDA counterparty) and G2 (filed BLA + fixed goal date); G3 is partial since peak-sales figures (JMP ~$750M US) exist but are analyst estimates, not company commitments. Score 7/ACTIVE is appropriate: real, specific, dated, asymmetric, but it is a heavily-watched lone clinical binary where approval is plausibly partly discounted and a CRL would be punishing.

verified.catalyst: onsemi (ON) agreed June 25, 2026 to acquire Synaptics in an all-stock merger at a fixed 1.350 ON-per-SYNA exchange ratio, targeted to close mid-2027.
verified.dated_milestone: Definitive merger agreement signed/announced 2026-06-25; expected close mid-2027 (next discrete date: S-4 effectiveness then a SYNA special shareholder vote, not yet scheduled, likely H2 2026).
verified.instrument: equity (SYNA common; merger-arb long SYNA / short 1.35x ON to lock the ratio)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty onsemi/ON) and G2 (concrete signed definitive agreement, fixed 1.350 ratio, $235M/$320M break fees) are met, but both are already public and PRICED. G3 fails: no specific currently-unpriced figure remains — the only quantifiable forward is the ~1.9% gross arb spread, which is the market's own pricing, not an unpriced edge.
verified.edge: L — catalyst is fired and re-rated (announced 4 days ago, SYNA +12% on the news). Gross spread is only ~1.87% (1.350 x ON $88.57 = $119.57 deal value vs SYNA $117.38, ~$2.19) to a ~12-month close, unannualized-thin, and a fixed-ratio all-stock deal gives no downside protection — holder carries full ON equity beta into close. Long-dated regulatory/FDI risk (mid-2027) further compresses risk-adjusted return.
verified.primary_source: SYNA/ON Form 8-K & Form 425 filings and StockTitan/TradingView coverage, dated 2026-06-25; live SYNA $117.38 / ON $88.57 quotes via FMP, 2026-06-29.
verified.analysis: The onsemi-Synaptics deal is a real, dated, named-counterparty merger (1.350 ON/share, signed 2026-06-25, close mid-2027) so it clears Bloom G1/G2, but it is an already-fired, fully-re-rated event with no unpriced forward figure (fails G3). The only remaining "edge" is a ~1.9% gross arb spread to a distant mid-2027 close on a fixed-ratio all-stock structure with no collar, meaning the position is dominated by ON's share-price beta rather than deal convergence — thin and unattractive on a risk-adjusted, annualized basis. Triage's read is correct: distant, fixed-ratio, thin-arb; this does not meet the strict forward/unpriced bar for an active catalyst.

verified.catalyst: UHS all-cash acquisition of Talkspace at $5.25/share is fully signed and shareholder-approved, with only state regulatory clearances left before a Q3 2026 close.
verified.dated_milestone: State regulatory approvals + deal close expected Q3 2026; shareholder vote completed 2026-05-29 (73.48% in favor). Next: state approvals land, then close.
verified.instrument: TALK common (long for merger-arb spread capture)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty UHS) + G2 (concrete $5.25/share all-cash commitment, board + shareholder approved)
verified.edge: L — spread already collapsed: TALK $5.20 vs $5.25 terminal = ~$0.05 / ~0.96% gross; only ~1-3mo to close, so annualized is low-double-digit at best and the catalyst is essentially priced in.
verified.primary_source: Talkspace 8-K / GlobeNewswire "Talkspace Stockholders Vote to Approve Acquisition by UHS" (2026-05-29); UHS/Talkspace definitive-agreement announcement (2026-03-09)
verified.analysis: Genuine, dated, unfired merger-arb: UHS signed a definitive $5.25/share all-cash deal for Talkspace (~$835M EV) on 2026-03-09, shareholders approved on 2026-05-29 (73.48%), and only state regulatory approvals remain before an expected Q3 2026 close — clears Bloom G1 (named counterparty) and G2 (concrete commitment). The catch is the edge is already gone: TALK trades $5.20 vs the $5.25 terminal, a ~0.96% gross spread, so the triage's "$5.25 vs $5.20" was the deal price vs the live quote (not a bump or competing bid), and the market has all but fully priced the close. Real and clean but thin — WATCH not ACTIVE, since a sub-1% spread on a low-break-risk strategic deal offers little incremental return for the residual state-regulatory and timing risk.

verified.catalyst: Definitive merger: Zymeworks (ZYME) acquires Theravance Biopharma for $17.00/share cash plus a CVR on ampreloxetine monetization; closing expected 2H 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: Shareholder vote (two-thirds) + HSR clearance; close expected 2H 2026 (no specific date set; agreement signed ~June 28-29, 2026)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Zymeworks) and G2 (concrete $17.00/share commitment) are satisfied, but G3 fails: there is no currently-UNPRICED figure to capture.
verified.edge: L — cash leg is fully priced/through; CVR is a speculative post-CYPRESS biology bet, not a clean spread.
verified.primary_source: PRNewswire / Theravance investor relations press release "Theravance Biopharma Enters Into Definitive Agreement To Be Acquired By Zymeworks For $17.00 Per Share In Cash Plus A Contingent Value Right" + SEC 8-K (filing 0001104659-26-078453), dated June 29, 2026; TBPH quote $17.06 via FMP June 29, 2026.
verified.analysis: The deal is real and definitive (G1 named counterparty Zymeworks, G2 concrete $17.00/share cash + CVR commitment, dated ~June 28-29 2026), but the merger-arb "cash with free-option kicker" thesis is already fired and re-rated: TBPH trades at $17.06, ABOVE the $17.00 cash price, so the cash spread is NEGATIVE (-0.35%) — there is no arb to capture, only downside if the CVR is valued at zero. The only forward, unpriced element is the ampreloxetine CVR (80% of net monetization over 10 years + $50M sale milestone + 10% net sales), but that is a speculative biology/business-development outcome on an asset whose pivotal CYPRESS data already read out March 3, 2026, not a clean dated catalyst — and the residual market premium above $17.00 suggests the tape is already ascribing some CVR value. The triage claim also had the direction inverted (it framed Zymeworks as the target being acquired; in fact Zymeworks is the acquirer of Theravance). G3 fails: no currently-unpriced spread figure exists, so this gates to NONE.

verified.catalyst: TruBridge (TBRG) to be acquired by IKS/Inventurus Knowledge Solutions for $26.25/share all-cash; HSR waiting period expired June 22 2026, special shareholder vote July 7 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: Special shareholder vote July 7, 2026; expected close Q3 2026
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty IKS/Inventurus) + G2 (signed merger agreement, $26.25 all-cash, ~27% shares locked via support agreements)
verified.edge: L — gross spread is only $0.07 ($26.18 vs $26.25 = ~0.27%), already fully re-rated; HSR cleared and vote is a near-formality, so essentially no unpriced upside remains, only deal-break downside.
verified.primary_source: TruBridge DEFM14A merger proxy (via StockTitan) + BusinessWire/8-K, HSR expiry June 22 2026, vote July 7 2026; FMP quote $26.18 (2026-06-29)
verified.analysis: The catalyst is real, specific, dated and well-documented: TBRG is being acquired by IKS (Inventurus Knowledge Solutions) for $26.25/share all-cash per the DEFM14A, with HSR cleared June 22 2026 and the special shareholder vote set for July 7 2026. However it FAILS the FORWARD/not-fired test — the stock is already at $26.18, a $0.07 (~0.27%) gross spread, so the catalyst is essentially fully priced with the major gate (HSR) already passed and ~27% of votes locked. No G3 (no specific unpriced figure remains); the only residual is deal-break risk, which is asymmetric downside. WATCH (not NONE) since the merger is genuine and dated, but score capped at 3 — no exploitable unpriced edge.
